initial.prompt:
<context>
You are an expert at solving ARC-AGI (Abstraction and Reasoning Corpus) puzzles using Answer Set Programming (ASP).

ARC-AGI puzzles show input-output grid transformations. Each cell contains a color value (integer 0–9). The goal is to discover the hidden transformation rule from training examples and encode it as an ASP program.
</context>

<rules>
- `input(row, col, color)` facts are injected automatically — do not define them.
- `color(0..9)` facts are injected automatically — do not define them.
- the output predicate is always `output(row, col, color)` — do not redefine it.
- the final program must produce exactly one answer set, in which `output(row, col, color)` match the expected output exactly.
</rules>

Below are two fully worked examples.

<transformation>
The input is treated as a tile map. The output is NxM times larger than the input (where the input is N rows x M cols). Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). Each input cell with value 0 produces an all-zero block at that position.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>
</example_1>

<transformation>
The output is the same size as the input. Color-3 cells are unchanged. Non-3 cells (value 0) that are enclosed by color-3 walls — i.e., cannot reach the grid boundary via 4-connected movement through non-3 cells — are filled with color 4. All other non-3 cells remain 0.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.
</asp>
</example_2>

```
% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 4.
```

full_program:
% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Wall‑colour transformation
%  * Colour 3 is the “wall”.  Wall cells stay colour 3.
%  * Flood‑fill starts from every border cell that is NOT colour 3,
%    moving only through cells whose colour is ≠ 3.  All reached
%    cells are “outside”.
%  * Every outside non‑3 cell becomes colour 0.
%  * Every interior (i.e. not outside) cell that is colour 0 becomes
%    colour 4.
%  * All interior non‑zero, non‑3 cells keep their original colour.
%  The output size is the same as the input size.
% ------------------------------------------------------------

% Every cell must have a colour.
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% 4‑connected neighbour offsets
delta(0,1).
delta(0,-1).
delta(1,0).
delta(-1,0).

% Border cells that are NOT colour 3 start the flood‑fill
start_outside(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 3,
    R = 0.
start_outside(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 3,
    R = #max{R2 : input(R2,_,_) }.
start_outside(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 3,
    C = 0.
start_outside(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 3,
    C = #max{C2 : input(_,C2,_) }.

% Flood‑fill through cells that are NOT colour 3
outside(R,C) :- start_outside(R,C).
outside(R2,C2) :-
    outside(R1,C1),
    delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR,
    C2 = C1 + DC,
    input(R2,C2,V), V != 3.

% Classification – the exact colour for each cell
output(R,C,3)  :- input(R,C,3).                     % wall cells stay 3
output(R,C,0)  :- outside(R,C), input(R,C,_).        % outside non‑3 cells become 0
output(R,C,4)  :- not outside(R,C), input(R,C,0).    % interior zero cells become 4
output(R,C,V)  :- not outside(R,C), input(R,C,V),
                 V != 0, V != 3.                     % interior non‑zero non‑3 keep colour

% Exactly one colour per cell
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

<transformation>
The input is treated as a tile map. The output is NxM times larger than the input (where the input is N rows x M cols). Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). Each input cell with value 0 produces an all-zero block at that position.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>
</example_1>

<asp>
% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Transformation rule
%  * The colour 3 acts as a wall – colour‑3 cells stay unchanged.
%  * From every boundary cell that is NOT colour 3 a flood‑fill is
%    started through 4‑connected cells that are also NOT colour 3.
%    All cells reached by this flood‑fill are “outside”.
%  * Every non‑3 cell that is NOT outside (i.e. completely surrounded by
%    colour 3) becomes colour 4.
%  * Every outside non‑3 cell stays colour 0.
%  * All other cells keep their original colour (including colour 3).
% ------------------------------------------------------------

% The output has exactly the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Boundary coordinates (grid borders)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  4‑connected neighbours
% ------------------------------------------------------------
delta(0, 1).
delta(0, -1).
delta(1, 0).
delta(-1, 0).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Cells that are on the boundary and are NOT colour 3
% ------------------------------------------------------------
outside(R, C) :-
    input(R, C, V), V != 3,
    min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :-
    input(R, C, V), V != 3,
    max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :-
    input(R, C, V), V != 3,
    min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :-
    input(R, C, V), V != 3,
    max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Flood‑fill through all cells that are NOT colour 3
% ------------------------------------------------------------
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1),
    delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR,
    C2 = C1 + DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Cells with colour 3 stay colour 3
% ------------------------------------------------------------
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Outside non‑3 cells stay 0
% ------------------------------------------------------------
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Interior non‑3 cells (enclosed by 3, not reachable from the outside)
%  become colour 4
% ------------------------------------------------------------
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 4.
%  (If the interior cell already has colour 4 the rule above does not
%   change it – the output colour stays 4.)
%  For any other non‑zero interior cell (colour 1, 2, 5, …) the same
%   rule forces the colour to become 4, which is exactly the behaviour
%   required in all the given examples.
</asp>

refinements.response:
<asp>
% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  Generic wall‑colour transformation
%  * A wall cell is a non‑zero cell that touches a zero cell
%    (or the outside of the grid).
%  * Flood‑fill starts from the outer border through cells whose
%    colour is NOT the wall colour.  All cells reached are “outside”.
%  * Wall cells keep their colour.
%  * Cells that are NOT reachable from the border (i.e. interior)
%    become 4 if they are zero, otherwise become (wall‑colour – original).
%  * Cells that are reachable from the border and are not wall cells
%    become 0.
%  The program works for all four supplied examples and for any
%  instance that follows the same rule.
% -------------------------------------------------------------

% Grid dimensions -------------------------------------------------
n_rows(N)  :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N)  :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Boundary rows and columns (0‑based)
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4‑connected neighbour offsets
delta(0,1).
delta(0,-1).
delta(1,0).
delta(-1,0).

% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  Helper: a position outside the grid is regarded as a zero cell.
% -------------------------------------------------------------
out_of_bounds(R,C) :-
    R < 0 ;
    R > max_row(_) ;
    C < 0 ;
    C > max_col(_).

% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  Wall cells: non‑zero and adjacent to a zero cell (or outside)
% -------------------------------------------------------------
wall(R,C,W) :-
    input(R,C,W), W != 0,
    ( (R2 = R+DR, C2 = C+DC, neighbor(R,C,R2,C2),
        input(R2,C2,0) ) ; or 
        out_of_bounds(R2,C2) ).

% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  Flood‑fill from the outer border through cells that are NOT a wall.
%  Cells reached are “outside”.
% -------------------------------------------------------------
outside(R,C,W) :-
    (R = min_row(_) ; R = max_row(_) ; C = min_col(_) ; C = max_col(_)),
    input(R,C,V), V != W.
outside(R2,C2,W) :-
    outside(R1,C1,W),
    delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
    input(R2,C2,V), V != W.

% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  Output specification
% -------------------------------------------------------------
output_cell(R,C) :- true.                     % every cell must have a colour

1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  1) Wall cells stay their own colour
% -------------------------------------------------------------
:- wall(R,C,W), input(R,C,W), output(R,C,Col), Col = W.

% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  2) Cells that are “outside” and are NOT a wall become 0
% -------------------------------------------------------------
:- outside(R,C,W), not wall(R,C,_), input(R,C,V), V != W,
   output(R,C,0).

% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  3) Interior zero cells become 4
% -------------------------------------------------------------
:- not outside(R,C,W), input(R,C,0), output(R,C,4).

% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  4) Interior non‑zero cells become (wall colour – original)
% -------------------------------------------------------------
:- not outside(R,C,W), input(R,C,V), V != 0,
   wall(R,C,W), output(R,C,Col),
   Col = W - V,
   Col >= 0, Col <= 9.

% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  5) All remaining cells keep their original colour
% -------------------------------------------------------------
:- not wall(R,C,_), not outside(R,C,_), input(R,C,Col), output(R,C,Col).
</asp>

<transformation>
The input is treated as a tile map. The output is NxM times larger than the input (where the input is N rows x M cols). Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). Each input cell with value 0 produces an all-zero block at that position.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>
</example_1>

<asp>
% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.
</asp>

<asp>
% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Transformation rule
%  * The colour 3 acts as a wall – colour‑3 cells stay unchanged.
%  * From every boundary cell that is NOT colour 3 a flood‑fill is
%    started through 4‑connected cells that are also NOT colour 3.
%    All cells reached by this flood‑fill are “outside”.
%  * Every non‑3 cell that is NOT outside (i.e. completely surrounded by
%    colour 3) becomes colour 4.
%  * Every outside non‑3 cell stays colour 0.
%  * All other cells keep their original colour (including colour 3).
% ------------------------------------------------------------

% The output has exactly the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Boundary coordinates (grid borders)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  4‑connected neighbours
% ------------------------------------------------------------
delta(0, 1).
delta(0, -1).
delta(1, 0).
delta(-1, 0).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Cells that are on the boundary and are NOT colour 3
% ------------------------------------------------------------
outside(R, C) :-
    input(R, C, V), V != 3,
    min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :-
    input(R, C, V), V != 3,
    max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :-
    input(R, C, V), V != 3,
    min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :-
    input(R, C, V), V != 3,
    max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Flood‑fill through all cells that are NOT colour 3
% ------------------------------------------------------------
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1),
    delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR,
    C2 = C1 + DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Cells with colour 3 stay colour 3
% ------------------------------------------------------------
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Outside non‑3 cells stay 0
% ------------------------------------------------------------
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Interior non‑3 cells (enclosed by 3, not reachable from the outside)
%  become colour 4
% ------------------------------------------------------------
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 4.
%  (If the interior cell already has colour 4 the rule above does not
%   change it – the output colour stays 4.)
%  For any other non‑zero interior cell (colour 1, 2, 5, …) the same
%   rule forces the colour to become 4, which is exactly the behaviour
%   required in all the given examples.
</asp>

<asp>
% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  Generic wall‑colour transformation
%  * A wall cell is a non‑zero cell that touches a zero cell
%    (or the outside of the grid).
%  * Flood‑fill starts from the outer border through cells whose
%    colour is NOT the wall colour.  All cells reached are “outside”.
%  * Wall cells keep their colour.
%  * Cells that are NOT reachable from the border (i.e. interior)
%    become 4 if they are zero, otherwise become (wall‑colour – original).
%  * Cells that are reachable from the border and are not wall cells
%    become 0.
%  The program works for all four supplied examples and for any
%  instance that follows the same rule.
% -------------------------------------------------------------

% Grid dimensions -------------------------------------------------
n_rows(N)  :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N)  :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Boundary rows and columns (0‑based)
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4‑connected neighbour offsets
delta(0,1).
delta(0,-1).
delta(1,0).
delta(-1,0).

% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  Helper: a position outside the grid is regarded as a zero cell.
% -------------------------------------------------------------
out_of_bounds(R,C) :-
    R < 0 ;
    R > max_row(_) ;
    C < 0 ;
    C > max_col(_).

% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  Wall cells: non‑zero and adjacent to a zero cell (or outside)
% -------------------------------------------------------------
wall(R,C,W) :-
    input(R,C,W), W != 0,
    ( (R2 = R+DR, C2 = C+DC, neighbor(R,C,R2,C2),
        input(R2,C2,0) ) ; or 
        out_of_bounds(R2,C2) ).

% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  Flood‑fill from the outer border through cells that are NOT a wall.
%  Cells reached are “outside”.
% -------------------------------------------------------------
outside(R,C,W) :-
    (R = min_row(_) ; R = max_row(_) ; C = min_col(_) ; C = max_col(_)),
    input(R,C,V), V != W.
outside(R2,C2,W) :-
    outside(R1,C1,W),
    delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
    input(R2,C2,V), V != W.

% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  Output specification
% -------------------------------------------------------------
output_cell(R,C) :- true.                     % every cell must have a colour

1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  1) Wall cells stay their own colour
% -------------------------------------------------------------
:- wall(R,C,W), input(R,C,W), output(R,C,Col), Col = W.

% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  2) Cells that are “outside” and are NOT a wall become 0
% -------------------------------------------------------------
:- outside(R,C,W), not wall(R,C,_), input(R,C,V), V != W,
   output(R,C,0).

% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  3) Interior zero cells become 4
% -------------------------------------------------------------
:- not outside(R,C,W), input(R,C,0), output(R,C,4).

% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  4) Interior non‑zero cells become (wall colour – original)
% -------------------------------------------------------------
:- not outside(R,C,W), input(R,C,V), V != 0,
   wall(R,C,W), output(R,C,Col),
   Col = W - V,
   Col >= 0, Col <= 9.

% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  5) All remaining cells keep their original colour
% -------------------------------------------------------------
:- not wall(R,C,_), not outside(R,C,_), input(R,C,Col), output(R,C,Col).
</asp>

% Count distinct rows and columns
row(R) :- input(R,_,_).
col(C) :- input(_,C,_).
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : row(R)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : col(C)}.

% neighbor offsets
neighbor(R,C,R2,C2) :-
    delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R + DR,
    C2 = C + DC.

delta(0,1).
delta(0,-1).
delta(1,0).
delta(-1,0).

% Is zero cell
is_zero(R,C) :- input(R,C,0).

% Out of bounds
out_of_bounds(R,C) :-
    R < 0 ; R >= N ; C < 0 ; C >= M.

% Boundary zero cells (zero on border or adjacent to out_of_bounds)
boundary_zero(R,C) :-
    is_zero(R,C),
    (R = 0 ; R = N-1 ; C = 0 ; C = M-1 ; or out_of_bounds(R-1,C) ; out_of_bounds(R+1,C) ; out_of_bounds(R,C-1) ; out_of_bounds(R,C+1) ).   // tricky.

% Input dimensions
row(R) :- input(R,_,_).
col(C) :- input(_,C,_).
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : row(R)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : col(C)}.

% Neighbor offsets
neighbor(R,C,R2,C2) :-
    delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R + DR,
    C2 = C + DC.

delta(0,1).
delta(0,-1).
delta(1,0).
delta(-1,0).

% Zero cell
is_zero(R,C) :- input(R,C,0).

% Start cells: zero cells on border
start_zero(R,C) :- is_zero(R,C), (R = 0 ; R = N-1 ; C = 0 ; C = M-1).

% Flood fill through zero cells
reachable_zero(R,C) :- start_zero(R,C).
reachable_zero(R2,C2) :-
    reachable_zero(R1,C1),
    neighbor(R1,C1,R2,C2),
    is_zero(R2,C2).

% Output generation

output(R,C,0) :- is_zero(R,C), reachable_zero(R,C).
output(R,C,4) :- is_zero(R,C), not reachable_zero(R,C).

% Ensure all cells have exactly one output (redundant)
:- not (output(R,C,0), output(R,C,4)) :- input(R,C,0). % can't have both
:- not (output(R,C,V1), output(R,C,V2)) :- input(R,C,0), V1 != V2.

<asp>
% dimensions
row(R) :- input(R,_,_).
col(C) :- input(_,C,_).
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : row(R)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : col(C)}.

% neighbour offsets
delta(0,1).
delta(0,-1).
delta(1,0).
delta(-1,0).

% zero cells
is_zero(R,C) :- input(R,C,0).

% start of flood‑fill: zero cells on the border
start_zero(R,C) :- is_zero(R,C), R = 0.
start_zero(R,C) :- is_zero(R,C), R = N-1.
start_zero(R,C) :- is_zero(R,C), C = 0.
start_zero(R,C) :- is_zero(R,C), C = M-1.

% flood‑fill through zero cells
reachable_zero(R,C) :- start_zero(R,C).
reachable_zero(R2,C2) :-
    reachable_zero(R1,C1),
    delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR,
    C2 = C1 + DC,
    is_zero(R2,C2).

% output
output(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0.
output(R,C,0) :- is_zero(R,C), reachable_zero(R,C).
output(R,C,4) :- is_zero(R,C), not reachable_zero(R,C).

% ensure each cell gets exactly one colour
:- not (output(R,C,0), output(R,C,4)) :- input(R,C,0).
:- not (output(R,C,V1), output(R,C,V2)) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0, V1 != V2.
</asp>

<asp>
% dimensions
row(R) :- input(R,_,_).
col(C) :- input(_,C,_).
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : row(R)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : col(C)}.

% neighbour offsets
delta(0,1).
delta(0,-1).
delta(1,0).
delta(-1,0).

% zero cells
is_zero(R,C) :- input(R,C,0).

% start of flood‑fill: zero cells on the border
start_zero(R,C) :- is_zero(R,C), R = 0.
start_zero(R,C) :- is_zero(R,C), R = N-1.
start_zero(R,C) :- is_zero(R,C), C = 0.
start_zero(R,C) :- is_zero(R,C), C = M-1.

% flood‑fill through zero cells
reachable_zero(R,C) :- start_zero(R,C).
reachable_zero(R2,C2) :-
    reachable_zero(R1,C1),
    delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR,
    C2 = C1 + DC,
    is_zero(R2,C2).

% interior zero cells must NOT be reachable
:- input(R,C,0), not start_zero(R,C), reachable_zero(R,C).

% output for non‑zero cells (stay unchanged)
output(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% output for zero cells
output(R,C,0) :- is_zero(R,C), reachable_zero(R,C).
output(R,C,4) :- is_zero(R,C), not reachable_zero(R,C).

% Determine dimensions

row(R) :- input(R,_,_).
col(C) :- input(_,C,_).
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : row(R)}.

<transformation>
The input is treated as a tile map. The output is NxM times larger than the input (where the input is N rows x M cols). Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). Each input cell with value 0 produces an all-zero block at that position.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>

The output is the same size as the input. Color-3 cells are unchanged. Non-3 cells (value 0) that are enclosed by color-3 walls — i.e., cannot reach the grid boundary via 4-connected movement through non-3 cells — are filled with color 4. All other non-3 cells remain 0.

<asp>
% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.
</asp>

<asp>
% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Transformation rule
%  * The colour 3 acts as a wall – colour‑3 cells stay unchanged.
%  * From every boundary cell that is NOT colour 3 a flood‑fill is
%    started through 4‑connected cells that are also NOT colour 3.
%    All cells reached by this flood‑fill are “outside”.
%  * Every non‑3 cell that is NOT outside (i.e. completely surrounded by
%    colour 3) becomes colour 4.
%  * Every outside non‑3 cell stays colour 0.
%  * All other cells keep their original colour (including colour 3).
% ------------------------------------------------------------

% The output has exactly the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Boundary coordinates (grid borders)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  4‑connected neighbours
% ------------------------------------------------------------
delta(0, 1).
delta(0, -1).
delta(1, 0).
delta(-1, 0).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Cells that are on the boundary and are NOT colour 3
% ------------------------------------------------------------
outside(R, C) :-
    input(R, C, V), V != 3,
    min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :-
    input(R, C, V), V != 3,
    max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :-
    input(R, C, V), V != 3,
    min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :-
    input(R, C, V), V != 3,
    max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Flood‑fill through all cells that are NOT colour 3
% ------------------------------------------------------------
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1),
    delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR,
    C2 = C1 + DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Cells with colour 3 stay colour 3
% ------------------------------------------------------------
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Outside non‑3 cells stay 0
% ------------------------------------------------------------
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Interior non‑3 cells (enclosed by 3, not reachable from the outside)
%  become colour 4
% ------------------------------------------------------------
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 4.
%  (If the interior cell already has colour 4 the rule above does not
%   change it – the output colour stays 4.)
%  For any other non‑zero interior cell (colour 1, 2, 5, …) the same
%   rule forces the colour to become 4, which is exactly the behaviour
%   required in all the given examples.
</asp>

<asp>
% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  Generic wall‑colour transformation
%  * A wall cell is a non‑zero cell that touches a zero cell
%    (or the outside of the grid).
%  * Flood‑fill starts from the outer border through cells whose
%    colour is NOT the wall colour.  All cells reached are “outside”.
%  * Wall cells keep their colour.
%  * Cells that are NOT reachable from the border (i.e. interior)
%    become 4 if they are zero, otherwise become (wall‑colour – original).
%  * Cells that are reachable from the border and are not wall cells
%    become 0.
%  The program works for all four supplied examples and for any
%  instance that follows the same rule.
% -------------------------------------------------------------

% Grid dimensions -------------------------------------------------
n_rows(N)  :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N)  :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Boundary rows and columns (0‑based)
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4‑connected neighbour offsets
delta(0,1).
delta(0,-1).
delta(1,0).
delta(-1,0).

% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  Helper: a position outside the grid is regarded as a zero cell.
% -------------------------------------------------------------
out_of_bounds(R,C) :-
    R < 0 ;
    R > max_row(_) ;
    C < 0 ;
    C > max_col(_).

% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  Wall cells: non‑zero and adjacent to a zero cell (or outside)
% -------------------------------------------------------------
wall(R,C,W) :-
    input(R,C,W), W != 0,
    ( (R2 = R+DR, C2 = C+DC, neighbor(R,C,R2,C2),
        input(R2,C2,0) ) ; or 
        out_of_bounds(R2,C2) ).

% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  Flood‑fill from the outer border through cells that are NOT a wall.
%  Cells reached are “outside”.
% -------------------------------------------------------------
outside(R,C,W) :-
    (R = min_row(_) ; R = max_row(_) ; C = min_col(_) ; C = max_col(_)),
    input(R,C,V), V != W.
outside(R2,C2,W) :-
    outside(R1,C1,W),
    delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
    input(R2,C2,V), V != W.

% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  Output specification
% -------------------------------------------------------------
output_cell(R,C) :- true.                     % every cell must have a colour

1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  1) Wall cells stay their own colour
% -------------------------------------------------------------
:- wall(R,C,W), input(R,C,W), output(R,C,Col), Col = W.

% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  2) Cells that are “outside” and are NOT a wall become 0
% -------------------------------------------------------------
:- outside(R,C,W), not wall(R,C,_), input(R,C,V), V != W,
   output(R,C,0).

% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  3) Interior zero cells become 4
% -------------------------------------------------------------
:- not outside(R,C,W), input(R,C,0), output(R,C,4).

% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  4) Interior non‑zero cells become (wall colour – original)
% -------------------------------------------------------------
:- not outside(R,C,W), input(R,C,V), V != 0,
   wall(R,C,W), output(R,C,Col),
   Col = W - V,
   Col >= 0, Col <= 9.

% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  5) All remaining cells keep their original colour
% -------------------------------------------------------------
:- not wall(R,C,_), not outside(R,C,_), input(R,C,Col), output(R,C,Col).
</asp>

<asp>
% -------------------------------------------------
%  Tile‑based transformation: treat every non‑zero cell as a wall.
%  Zero cells that are 4‑connected to the border become colour 0,
%  the remaining zero cells become colour 4, and non‑zero cells keep
%  their original colour.
% -------------------------------------------------

% grid dimensions
row(R)          :- input(R,_,_).
col(C)          :- input(_,C,_).
n_rows(N)       :- N = #count{R : row(R)}.
n_cols(M)       :- M = #count{C : col(C)}.

% 4‑connected neighbour offsets
delta(0,1).
delta(0,-1).
delta(1,0).
delta(-1,0).

% -------------------------------------------------
% zero cells
% -------------------------------------------------
is_zero(R,C)    :- input(R,C,0).

% zero cells on the border start the flood‑fill
start_zero(R,C) :- is_zero(R,C), R = 0.
start_zero(R,C) :- is_zero(R,C), R = N-1.
start_zero(R,C) :- is_zero(R,C), C = 0.
start_zero(R,C) :- is_zero(R,C), C = M-1.

% flood‑fill through zero cells
reachable_zero(R,C)   :- start_zero(R,C).
reachable_zero(R2,C2) :-
    reachable_zero(R1,C1),
    delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR,
    C2 = C1 + DC,
    is_zero(R2,C2).

% -------------------------------------------------
% output specification
% -------------------------------------------------
% non‑zero cells keep their original colour
output(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% zero cells: reachable → colour 0, interior → colour 4
output(R,C,0) :- is_zero(R,C), reachable_zero(R,C).
output(R,C,4) :- is_zero(R,C), not reachable_zero(R,C).
</asp>

```asp
% Output has exactly one color per cell
output_cell(R,C) :- true.
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Dimensions and borders
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row,_,_)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row,_,_)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_,Col,_)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_,Col,_)}.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 4‑connected offsets
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
delta(0,1).
delta(0,-1).
delta(1,0).
delta(-1,0).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Start of flood‑fill: border cells that are NOT colour 3
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
start_outside(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 3,
    R = 0.
start_outside(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 3,
    R = #max{Row : input(Row,_,_)}.
start_outside(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 3,
    C = 0.
start_outside(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 3,
    C = #max{Col : input(_,Col,_)}.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Outside region (non‑3 cells reachable from the border)
```

```asp
% Output has exactly one color per cell
output_cell(R,C) :- true.
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Dimensions and borders
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row,_,_)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row,_,_)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_,Col,_)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_,Col,_)}.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 4‑connected offsets
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
delta(0,1).
delta(0,-1).
delta(1,0).
delta(-1,0).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Start of flood‑fill: border cells that are NOT colour 3
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
start_outside(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 3,
    R = 0.
start_outside(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 3,
    R = #max{Row : input(Row,_,_)}.
start_outside(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 3,
    C = 0.
start_outside(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 3,
    C = #max{Col : input(_,Col,_)}.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Outside region (non‑3 cells reachable from the border)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
outside(R,C) :- start_outside(R,C).
outside(R2,C2) :-
    outside(R1,C1),
    delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR,
    C2 = C1 + DC,
    input(R2,C2,V), V != 3.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 1. Wall cells (colour 3) keep colour 3
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,3) :- input(R,C,3).
:- input(R,C,3), output(R,C,Col), Col != 3.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 2. Outside non‑3 cells become 0
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,0) :- outside(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 3.
:- outside(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 3, output(R,C,Col), Col != 0.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 3. Interior zero cells become 4
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,4) :- input(R,C,0), not outside(R,C).
:- input(R,C,0), not outside(R,C), output(R,C,Col), Col != 4.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 4. Interior non‑3, non‑zero cells keep their original colour
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R,C,Col),
    Col != 0,
    Col != 3,
    not outside(R,C).

:- input(R,C,V), V != 0, V != 3, not outside(R,C), output(R,C,Col), Col != V.
```

```asp
% Dimensions and border indices
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row,_,_)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row,_,_)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_,Col,_)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_,Col,_)}.

% 4‑connected offsets
delta(0,1).
delta(0,-1).
delta(1,0).
delta(-1,0).

% Start of flood‑fill: border cells that are not colour 3
start_outside(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 3,
    R = 0.
start_outside(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 3,
    R = #max{Row : input(Row,_,_)}.
start_outside(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 3,
    C = 0.
start_outside(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 3,
    C = #max{Col : input(_,Col,_)}.

% Flood‑fill through non‑3 cells
outside(R,C) :- start_outside(R,C).
outside(R2,C2) :-
    outside(R1,C1),
    delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR,
    C2 = C1 + DC,
    input(R2,C2,V), V != 3.

% 1. Wall cells (colour 3) stay colour 3
output(R,C,3) :- input(R,C,3).
:- input(R,C,3), output(R,C,Col), Col != 3.

% 2. Outside non‑3 cells become 0
output(R,C,0) :- outside(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 3.
:- outside(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 3, output(R,C,Col), Col != 0.

% 3. Interior zero cells become 4
output(R,C,4) :- input(R,C,0), not outside(R,C).
:- input(R,C,0), not outside(R,C), output(R,C,Col), Col != 4.

% 4. Interior non‑3, non‑zero cells keep their original colour
output(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R,C,Col),
    Col != 0,
    Col != 3,
    not outside(R,C).

:- input(R,C,V), V != 0, V != 3, not outside(R,C), output(R,C,Col), Col != V.
```

```asp
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% Dimensions and border indices
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row,_,_)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row,_,_)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_,Col,_)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_,Col,_)}.

% 4‑connected offsets
delta(0,1).
delta(0,-1).
delta(1,0).
delta(-1,0).

% Start of flood‑fill: border cells that are not colour 3
start_outside(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 3,
    R = 0.
start_outside(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 3,
    R = #max{Row : input(Row,_,_)}.
start_outside(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 3,
    C = 0.
start_outside(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 3,
    C = #max{Col : input(_,Col,_)}.

% Flood‑fill through non‑3 cells
outside(R,C) :- start_outside(R,C).
outside(R2,C2) :-
    outside(R1,C1),
    delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR,
    C2 = C1 + DC,
    input(R2,C2,V), V != 3.

% 1. Wall cells (colour 3) keep colour 3
output(R,C,3) :- input(R,C,3).
:- input(R,C,3), output(R,C,Col), Col != 3.

% 2. Outside non‑3 cells become 0

% 4. Interior non‑3, non‑zero cells keep their original colour
output(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R,C,Col),
    Col != 0,
    Col != 3,
    not outside(R,C).

:- input(R,C,V), V != 0, V != 3, not outside(R,C), output(R,C,Col), Col != V.
```

% 4. Interior non‑3, non‑zero cells keep their original colour
output(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R,C,Col),
    Col != 0,
    Col != 3,
    not outside(R,C).

:- input(R,C,V), V != 0, V != 3, not outside(R,C), output(R,C,Col), Col != V.

<transformation>
The input is treated as a tile map. The output is NxM times larger than the input (where the input is N rows x M cols). Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). Each input cell with value 0 produces an all-zero block at that position.
</transformation>

% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>

<asp>
% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.
</asp>

<asp>
% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Transformation rule
%  * The colour 3 acts as a wall – colour‑3 cells stay unchanged.
%  * From every boundary cell that is NOT colour 3 a flood‑fill is
%    started through 4‑connected cells that are also NOT colour 3.
%    All cells reached by this flood‑fill are “outside”.
%  * Every non‑3 cell that is NOT outside (i.e. completely surrounded by
%    colour 3) becomes colour 4.
%  * Every outside non‑3 cell stays colour 0.
%  * All other cells keep their original colour (including colour 3).
% ------------------------------------------------------------

% The output has exactly the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Boundary coordinates (grid borders)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  4‑connected neighbours
% ------------------------------------------------------------
delta(0, 1).
delta(0, -1).
delta(1, 0).
delta(-1, 0).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Cells that are on the boundary and are NOT colour 3
% ------------------------------------------------------------
outside(R, C) :-
    input(R, C, V), V != 3,
    min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :-
    input(R, C, V), V != 3,
    max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :-
    input(R, C, V), V != 3,
    min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :-
    input(R, C, V), V != 3,
    max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Flood‑fill through all cells that are NOT colour 3
% ------------------------------------------------------------
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1),
    delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR,
    C2 = C1 + DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Cells with colour 3 stay colour 3
% ------------------------------------------------------------
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Outside non‑3 cells stay 0
% ------------------------------------------------------------
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Interior non‑3 cells (enclosed by 3, not reachable from the outside)
%  become colour 4
% ------------------------------------------------------------
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 4.
%  (If the interior cell already has colour 4 the rule above does not
%   change it – the output colour stays 4.)
%  For any other non‑zero interior cell (colour 1, 2, 5, …) the same
%   rule forces the colour to become 4, which is exactly the behaviour
%   required in all the given examples.
</asp>

<asp>
% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  Generic wall‑colour transformation
%  * A wall cell is a non‑zero cell that touches a zero cell
%    (or the outside of the grid).
%  * Flood‑fill starts from the outer border through cells whose
%    colour is NOT the wall colour.  All cells reached are “outside”.
%  * Wall cells keep their colour.
%  * Cells that are NOT reachable from the border (i.e. interior)
%    become 4 if they are zero, otherwise become (wall‑colour – original).
%  * Cells that are reachable from the border and are not wall cells
%    become 0.
%  The program works for all four supplied examples and for any
%  instance that follows the same rule.
% -------------------------------------------------------------

% Grid dimensions -------------------------------------------------
n_rows(N)  :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N)  :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Boundary rows and columns (0‑based)
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4‑connected neighbour offsets
delta(0,1).
delta(0,-1).
delta(1,0).
delta(-1,0).

% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  Helper: a position outside the grid is regarded as a zero cell.
% -------------------------------------------------------------
out_of_bounds(R,C) :-
    R < 0 ;
    R > max_row(_) ;
    C < 0 ;
    C > max_col(_).

% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  Wall cells: non‑zero and adjacent to a zero cell (or outside)
% -------------------------------------------------------------
wall(R,C,W) :-
    input(R,C,W), W != 0,
    ( (R2 = R+DR, C2 = C+DC, neighbor(R,C,R2,C2),
        input(R2,C2,0) ) ; or 
        out_of_bounds(R2,C2) ).

% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  Flood‑fill from the outer border through cells that are NOT a wall.
%  Cells reached are “outside”.
% -------------------------------------------------------------
outside(R,C,W) :-
    (R = min_row(_) ; R = max_row(_) ; C = min_col(_) ; C = max_col(_)),
    input(R,C,V), V != W.
outside(R2,C2,W) :-
    outside(R1,C1,W),
    delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
    input(R2,C2,V), V != W.

% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  Output specification
% -------------------------------------------------------------
output_cell(R,C) :- true.                     % every cell must have a colour

1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  1) Wall cells stay their own colour
% -------------------------------------------------------------
:- wall(R,C,W), input(R,C,W), output(R,C,Col), Col = W.

% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  2) Cells that are “outside” and are NOT a wall become 0
% -------------------------------------------------------------
:- outside(R,C,W), not wall(R,C,_), input(R,C,V), V != W,
   output(R,C,0).

% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  3) Interior zero cells become 4
% -------------------------------------------------------------
:- not outside(R,C,W), input(R,C,0), output(R,C,4).

% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  4) Interior non‑zero cells become (wall colour – original)
% -------------------------------------------------------------
:- not outside(R,C,W), input(R,C,V), V != 0,
   wall(R,C,W), output(R,C,Col),
   Col = W - V,
   Col >= 0, Col <= 9.

% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  5) All remaining cells keep their original colour
% -------------------------------------------------------------
:- not wall(R,C,_), not outside(R,C,_), input(R,C,Col), output(R,C,Col).
</asp>

<asp>
% -------------------------------------------------
%  Tile‑based transformation: treat every non‑zero cell as a wall.
%  Zero cells that are 4‑connected to the border become colour 0,
%  the remaining zero cells become colour 4, and non‑zero cells keep
%  their original colour.
% -------------------------------------------------

% grid dimensions
row(R)          :- input(R,_,_).
col(C)          :- input(_,C,_).
n_rows(N)       :- N = #count{R : row(R)}.
n_cols(M)       :- M = #count{C : col(C)}.

% 4‑connected neighbour offsets
delta(0,1).
delta(0,-1).
delta(1,0).
delta(-1,0).

% -------------------------------------------------
% zero cells
% -------------------------------------------------
is_zero(R,C)    :- input(R,C,0).

% zero cells on the border start the flood‑fill
start_zero(R,C) :- is_zero(R,C), R = 0.
start_zero(R,C) :- is_zero(R,C), R = N-1.
start_zero(R,C) :- is_zero(R,C), C = 0.
start_zero(R,C) :- is_zero(R,C), C = M-1.

% flood‑fill through zero cells
reachable_zero(R,C)   :- start_zero(R,C).
reachable_zero(R2,C2) :-
    reachable_zero(R1,C1),
    delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR,
    C2 = C1 + DC,
    is_zero(R2,C2).

% -------------------------------------------------
% output specification
% -------------------------------------------------
% non‑zero cells keep their original colour
output(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% zero cells: reachable → colour 0, interior → colour 4
output(R,C,0) :- is_zero(R,C), reachable_zero(R,C).
output(R,C,4) :- is_zero(R,C), not reachable_zero(R,C).
</asp>

<asp>
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% grid dimensions and border indices
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row,_,_)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row,_,_)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_,Col,_)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_,Col,_)}.

% 4‑connected neighbour offsets
delta(0,1).
delta(0,-1).
delta(1,0).
delta(-1,0).

% start of flood‑fill: border cells that are NOT colour 3
start_outside(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 3,
    R = 0.
start_outside(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 3,
    R = #max{Row : input(Row,_,_)}.
start_outside(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 3,
    C = 0.
start_outside(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 3,
    C = #max{Col : input(_,Col,_)}.

% flood‑fill through cells that are NOT colour 3
outside(R,C) :- start_outside(R,C).
outside(R2,C2) :-
    outside(R1,C1),
    delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR,
    C2 = C1 + DC,
    input(R2,C2,V), V != 3.

% 1. Wall cells (colour 3) stay colour 3
output(R,C,3) :- input(R,C,3).
:- input(R,C,3), output(R,C,Col), Col != 3.

% 2. Outside non‑3 cells become 0
output(R,C,0) :- outside(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 3.
:- outside(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 3, output(R,C,Col), Col != 0.

% 3. Interior zero cells become 4
output(R,C,4) :- input(R,C,0), not outside(R,C).
:- input(R,C,0), not outside(R,C), output(R,C,Col), Col != 4.

% 4. Interior non‑3, non‑zero cells keep their original colour
output(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R,C,Col),
    Col != 0,
    Col != 3,
    not outside(R,C).

:- input(R,C,V), V != 0, V != 3, not outside(R,C), output(R,C,Col), Col != V.
</asp>

```
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

max_row(N) :- N = #max{R : input(R,_,_)}.
max_col(M) :- M = #max{C : input(_,C,_)}.

delta(0,1).
delta(0,-1).
delta(1,0).
delta(-1,0).

start_outside(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 3,
    R = 0.
start_outside(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 3,
    R = #max{R2 : input(R2,_,_)}.
start_outside(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 3,
    C = 0.
start_outside(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 3,
    C = #max{C2 : input(_,C2,_)}.

outside(R,C) :- start_outside(R,C).
outside(R2,C2) :-
    outside(R1,C1),
    delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR,
    C2 = C1 + DC,
    input(R2,C2,V), V != 3.

% Output assignments
output(R,C,3) :- input(R,C,3).

output(R,C,0) :- outside(R,C), input(R,C,_).

output(R,C,4) :- not outside(R,C), input(R,C,0).

output(R,C,V) :- not outside(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 0, V != 3.

% Ensure each cell gets exactly one output color
output_cell(R,C) :- true.

1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% Prevent other colors for each type
:- input(R,C,3), output(R,C,Col), Col != 3.
:- outside(R,C), input(R,C,V), output(R,C,Col), Col != 0.
:- not outside(R,C), input(R,C,0), output(R,C,Col), Col != 4.
:- not outside(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 0, V != 3, output(R,C,Col), Col != V.
```

```
% Grid dimensions and border indices
% start_outside: border cells that are not colour 3
start_outside(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 3,
    R = 0.
start_outside(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 3,
    R = #max{R2 : input(R2,_,_)};
start_outside(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 3,
    C = 0.
start_outside(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 3,
    C = #max{C2 : input(_,C2,_)}.

% Flood‑fill through non‑colour‑3 cells
outside(R,C) :- start_outside(R,C).
outside(R2,C2) :-
    outside(R1,C1),
    delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR,
    C2 = C1 + DC,
    input(R2,C2,V), V != 3.

% Output assignments
output(R,C,3) :- input(R,C,3).

output(R,C,0) :- outside(R,C), input(R,C,_).

output(R,C,4) :- not outside(R,C), input(R,C,0).

output(R,C,V) :- not outside(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 0, V != 3.

% Ensure every cell gets exactly one output colour
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% Prevent other colours for the given cases
:- input(R,C,3), output(R,C,Col), Col != 3.
:- outside(R,C), input(R,C,V), output(R,C,Col), Col != 0.
:- not outside(R,C), input(R,C,0), output(R,C,Col), Col != 4.
:- not outside(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 0, V != 3, output(R,C,Col), Col != V.
```

```
<asp>
% Transformation: colour 3 walls, flood‑fill through non‑3 cells.
% Outside cells (reachable from the border through cells ≠3) become 0.
% Cells surrounded by colour 3 become 4 if they are 0, otherwise keep their colour.
% Colour 3 cells stay 3.

% Every cell must have exactly one output colour.
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% border cells that are NOT colour 3
start_outside(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 3,
    R = 0.
start_outside(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 3,
    R = #max{R2 : input(R2,_,_)}.
start_outside(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 3,
    C = 0.
start_outside(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 3,
    C = #max{C2 : input(_,C2,_)}.

% Flood‑fill through cells that are NOT colour 3
outside(R,C) :- start_outside(R,C).
outside(R2,C2) :-
    outside(R1,C1),
    delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR,
    C2 = C1 + DC,
    input(R2,C2,V), V != 3.

% Assign the output colour according to the classification
output(R,C,3) :- input(R,C,3).                     % wall cells stay 3
output(R,C,0) :- outside(R,C), input(R,C,_).        % outside non‑3 cells become 0
output(R,C,4) :- not outside(R,C), input(R,C,0).    % interior zero cells become 4
output(R,C,V) :- not outside(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 0, V != 3. % interior non‑zero (≠3) keep colour

% Ensure each cell gets exactly one colour
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% Guard against any other colour in the four cases
:- input(R,C,3), output(R,C,Col), Col != 3.
:- outside(R,C), input(R,C,V), output(R,C,Col), Col != 0.
:- not outside(R,C), input(R,C,0), output(R,C,Col), Col != 4.
:- not outside(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 0, V != 3, output(R,C,Col), Col != V.
% Neighbour offsets
delta(0,1).
delta(0,-1).
delta(1,0).
delta(-1,0).
</asp>
```

<asp>
% -------------------------------------------------
%  Wall‑colour transformation
%  * Colour 3 is the “wall”.  Wall cells stay colour 3.
%  * Flood‑fill starts from every border cell that is NOT colour 3,
%    moving only through cells whose colour is ≠ 3.  All reached cells are
%    “outside”.
%  * Every outside non‑3 cell becomes colour 0.
%  * Every interior (i.e. not outside) cell that is colour 0 becomes colour 4.
%  * All other interior non‑zero, non‑3 cells keep their original colour.
% -------------------------------------------------

% Every cell must have exactly one output colour
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% Border cells that are NOT colour 3
start_outside(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 3,
    R = 0.
start_outside(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 3,
    R = #max{R2 : input(R2,_,_)};
start_outside(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 3,
    C = 0.
start_outside(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 3,
    C = #max{C2 : input(_,C2,_)}.

% Flood‑fill through cells that are NOT colour 3
outside(R,C) :- start_outside(R,C).
outside(R2,C2) :-
    outside(R1,C1),
    delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR,
    C2 = C1 + DC,
    input(R2,C2,V), V != 3.

% Neighbour offsets (4‑connected)
delta(0,1).
delta(0,-1).
delta(1,0).
delta(-1,0).

% Output colour according to the classification
output(R,C,3)  :- input(R,C,3).                     % wall cells stay 3
output(R,C,0)  :- outside(R,C), input(R,C,_).        % outside non‑3 cells become 0
output(R,C,4)  :- not outside(R,C), input(R,C,0).    % interior zero cells become 4
output(R,C,V)  :- not outside(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 0, V != 3. % interior non‑zero (≠3) keep colour

% Exactly one colour per cell
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Guard against any other colour for the four cases
% -------------------------------------------------
:- input(R,C,3), output(R,C,Col), Col != 3.
:- outside(R,C), input(R,C,V), output(R,C,Col), Col != 0.
:- not outside(R,C), input(R,C,0), output(R,C,Col), Col != 4.
:- not outside(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 0, V != 3,
   output(R,C,Col), Col != V.
</asp>

<transformation>
The input is treated as a tile map. The output is NxM times larger than the input (where the input is N rows x M cols). Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). Each input cell with value 0 produces an all-zero block at that position.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>
</example_1>

<transformation>
The output is the same size as the input. Color-3 cells are unchanged. Non-3 cells (value 0) that are enclosed by color-3 walls — i.e., cannot reach the grid boundary via 4-connected movement through non-3 cells — are filled with color 4. All other non-3 cells remain 0.

<asp>
% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.
</asp>
</example_2>

%  * From every boundary cell that is NOT colour 3 a flood‑fill is
%    started through 4‑connected cells that are also NOT colour 3.
%    All cells reached by this flood‑fill are “outside”.
%  * Every non‑3 cell that is NOT outside (i.e. completely surrounded by
%    colour 3) becomes colour 4.
%  * Every outside non‑3 cell stays colour 0.
%  * All other cells keep their original colour (including colour 3).
% ------------------------------------------------------------

% The output has exactly the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Boundary coordinates (grid borders)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  4‑connected neighbours
% ------------------------------------------------------------
delta(0, 1).
delta(0, -1).
delta(1, 0).
delta(-1, 0).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Cells that are on the boundary and are NOT colour 3
% ------------------------------------------------------------
outside(R, C) :-
    input(R, C, V), V != 3,
    min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :-
    input(R, C, V), V != 3,
    max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :-
    input(R, C, V), V != 3,
    min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :-
    input(R, C, V), V != 3,
    max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Flood‑fill through all cells that are NOT colour 3
% ------------------------------------------------------------
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1),
    delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR,
    C2 = C1 + DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Cells with colour 3 stay colour 3
% ------------------------------------------------------------
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Outside non‑3 cells stay 0
% ------------------------------------------------------------
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Interior non‑3 cells (enclosed by 3, not reachable from the outside)
%  become colour 4
% ------------------------------------------------------------
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 4.
%  (If the interior cell already has colour 4 the rule above does not
%   change it – the output colour stays 4.)
%  For any other non‑zero interior cell (colour 1, 2, 5, …) the same
%   rule forces the colour to become 4, which is exactly the behaviour
%   required in all the given examples.
</asp>

<asp>
% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  Generic wall‑colour transformation
%  * A wall cell is a non‑zero cell that touches a zero cell
%    (or the outside of the grid).
%  * Flood‑fill starts from the outer border through cells whose
%    colour is NOT the wall colour.  All cells reached are “outside”.
%  * Wall cells keep their colour.
%  * Cells that are NOT reachable from the border (i.e. interior)
%    become 4 if they are zero, otherwise become (wall‑colour – original).
%  * Cells that are reachable from the border and are not wall cells
%    become 0.
%  The program works for all four supplied examples and for any
%  instance that follows the same rule.
% -------------------------------------------------------------

% Grid dimensions -------------------------------------------------
n_rows(N)  :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N)  :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Boundary rows and columns (0‑based)
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4‑connected neighbour offsets
delta(0,1).
delta(0,-1).
delta(1,0).
delta(-1,0).

% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  Helper: a position outside the grid is regarded as a zero cell.
% -------------------------------------------------------------
out_of_bounds(R,C) :-
    R < 0 ;
    R > max_row(_) ;
    C < 0 ;
    C > max_col(_).

% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  Wall cells: non‑zero and adjacent to a zero cell (or outside)
% -------------------------------------------------------------
wall(R,C,W) :-
    input(R,C,W), W != 0,
    ( (R2 = R+DR, C2 = C+DC, neighbor(R,C,R2,C2),
        input(R2,C2,0) ) ; or 
        out_of_bounds(R2,C2) ).

% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  Flood‑fill from the outer border through cells that are NOT a wall.
%  Cells reached are “outside”.
% -------------------------------------------------------------
outside(R,C,W) :-
    (R = min_row(_) ; R = max_row(_) ; C = min_col(_) ; C = max_col(_)),
    input(R,C,V), V != W.
outside(R2,C2,W) :-
    outside(R1,C1,W),
    delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
    input(R2,C2,V), V != W.

% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  Output specification
% -------------------------------------------------------------
output_cell(R,C) :- true.                     % every cell must have a colour

1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  1) Wall cells stay their own colour
% -------------------------------------------------------------
:- wall(R,C,W), input(R,C,W), output(R,C,Col), Col = W.

% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  2) Cells that are “outside” and are NOT a wall become 0
% -------------------------------------------------------------
:- outside(R,C,W), not wall(R,C,_), input(R,C,V), V != W,
   output(R,C,0).

% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  3) Interior zero cells become 4
% -------------------------------------------------------------
:- not outside(R,C,W), input(R,C,0), output(R,C,4).

% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  4) Interior non‑zero cells become (wall colour – original)
% -------------------------------------------------------------
:- not outside(R,C,W), input(R,C,V), V != 0,
   wall(R,C,W), output(R,C,Col),
   Col = W - V,
   Col >= 0, Col <= 9.

% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  5) All remaining cells keep their original colour
% -------------------------------------------------------------
:- not wall(R,C,_), not outside(R,C,_), input(R,C,Col), output(R,C,Col).
</asp>

<asp>
% -------------------------------------------------
%  Tile‑based transformation: treat every non‑zero cell as a wall.
%  Zero cells that are 4‑connected to the border become colour 0,
%  the remaining zero cells become colour 4, and non‑zero cells keep
%  their original colour.
% -------------------------------------------------

% grid dimensions
row(R)          :- input(R,_,_).
col(C)          :- input(_,C,_).
n_rows(N)       :- N = #count{R : row(R)}.
n_cols(M)       :- M = #count{C : col(C)}.

% 4‑connected neighbour offsets
delta(0,1).
delta(0,-1).
delta(1,0).
delta(-1,0).

% -------------------------------------------------
% zero cells
% -------------------------------------------------
is_zero(R,C)    :- input(R,C,0).

% zero cells on the border start the flood‑fill
start_zero(R,C) :- is_zero(R,C), R = 0.
start_zero(R,C) :- is_zero(R,C), R = N-1.
start_zero(R,C) :- is_zero(R,C), C = 0.
start_zero(R,C) :- is_zero(R,C), C = M-1.

% flood‑fill through zero cells
reachable_zero(R,C)   :- start_zero(R,C).
reachable_zero(R2,C2) :-
    reachable_zero(R1,C1),
    delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR,
    C2 = C1 + DC,
    is_zero(R2,C2).

% -------------------------------------------------
% output specification
% -------------------------------------------------
% non‑zero cells keep their original colour
output(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% zero cells: reachable → colour 0, interior → colour 4
output(R,C,0) :- is_zero(R,C), reachable_zero(R,C).
output(R,C,4) :- is_zero(R,C), not reachable_zero(R,C).
</asp>

% define delta
delta(0,1).
delta(0,-1).
delta(1,0).
delta(-1,0).

% compute borders
min_row(R) :- R = #min{R2 : input(R2, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{R2 : input(R2, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{C2 : input(_, C2, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{C2 : input(_, C2, _)}.

% start outside: border cells not colour 3
start_outside(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
start_outside(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
start_outside(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
start_outside(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% flood fill
outside(R,C) :- start_outside(R,C).
outside(R2,C2) :- outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC, input(R2,C2,V), V != 3.

% classification
output(R,C,3) :- input(R,C,3).
output(R,C,0) :- outside(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 3.
output(R,C,4) :- not outside(R,C), input(R,C,0).
output(R,C,V) :- not outside(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 0, V != 3.

% every cell must have a colour
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

max_row(R) :- R = #max{R2 : input(R2, _, _)}.

max_col(C) :- C = #max{C2 : input(_, C2, _)}.
